title: Annex XI to AMLA SPD 2026-2028
text:
ANNEX XI PLANNING ON RTS/ITS/GL
ANNEX XI PLANNING on RTS/ITS/GL
Texts Articles Title Type Consultation Final Draft
Phase
AMLA-R 12(7) Direct supervision selection process: minimum activities for determining RTS N/A Q4 2025
when a credit or financial institution operates in a Member State other
than where it is established and the methodology for classifying risk pro-
files.
AMLD 40(2) FS Benchmarks and methodology for assessing and classifying the risk pro- RTS N/A Q4 2025
file of obliged entities and the frequency of reviews of that risk profile
(financial sector).
AMLA-R 15(3) Cooperation for the purposes of direct supervision including conditions ITS Q1 2026 Q3 2026
under which financial supervisors assist AMLA, the periodic risk assess-
ment process and the respective roles of supervisors and AMLA, working
arrangements for the transfer of supervisory tasks, procedures for prep-
aration and adoption of decisions and rules and arrangements for joint
supervisory teams.
AMLD 53(10) Indicators to classify the level and gravity of breaches of AML/CFT obliga- RTS Additional Q3 2026
tions, criteria to be taken into account when determining pecuniary NFS edition
sanctions or administrative measures, and a methodology for the appli-
Q1 2026
cation of periodic penalty payments.
98

ANNEX XI PLANNING ON RTS/ITS/GL
Texts Articles Title Type Consultation Final Draft
Phase
AMLR 16(4) Minimum requirements of group-wide policies including minimum RTS Q2 2026 Q3 2026
standards for information sharing, criteria for identifying the parent un-
dertaking and the conditions under which group-wide requirements ap-
ply to entities that are part of structures, and the criteria for identifying
the parent undertaking in the Union in those cases.
AMLR 19(9) Higher-risk sectors and associated occasional transaction thresholds, RTS Q2 2026 Q3 2026
and measures for identifying occasional transactions and business rela-
tionships.
AMLR 28(1) Information required for performing customer due diligence. RTS Q2 2026 Q3 2026
AMLA-R 11(6) Central AML/CFT database: procedure, formats and timelines for the RTS Q2 2026 Q3 2026
transmission of information, scope and level of detail of information,
type of information to be disclosed, information requiring prior approval,
materiality of breaches for submission of information, conditions for di-
recting additional requests, and types of additional information to be
transmitted to AMLA
AMLA-R 41(2) Format to be used by AMLA for reporting information, including the re- ITS Q2 2026 Q4 2026
sults of joint analyses, to the EPPO - in consultation with EPPO.
99

ANNEX XI PLANNING ON RTS/ITS/GL
Texts Articles Title Type Consultation Final Draft
Phase
AMLR 17(3) Additional measures for branches and subsidiaries in a third country RTS Q2 2026 Q3 2026
where the law of that third country does not permit compliance with
AMLR, and related supervisory actions.
AMLD 46(4) Duties of the home and host supervisors and the modalities of coopera- RTS Q2 2026 Q3 2026
tion between them.
AMLD 53(11) Base amounts of pecuniary sanctions relative to turnover, per type of GL Q2 2026 Q4 2026
breach and category of obliged entity.
AMLR 81(1) Format for dissemination of information from FIUs to EPPO - in consulta- ITS Q2 2026 Q3 2026
tion with EPPO.
AMLD 31(2) Format for the exchange of information among FIUs. ITS Q2 2026 Q3 2026
AMLD 31(3) Relevance and selection criteria for FIUs to determine whether a suspi- RTS Q2 2026 Q4 2026
cious transaction report concerns another Member State.
AMLR 10(4) Minimum requirements for the content of the business-wide risk assess- GL Q3 2026 Q4 2026
ment
AMLR 20(3) Risk variables and risk factors when entering into business relationships GL Q3 2026 Q4 2026
or carrying out occasional transactions.
100

ANNEX XI PLANNING ON RTS/ITS/GL
Texts Articles Title Type Consultation Final Draft
Phase
AMLR 26(5) Ongoing monitoring of a business relationship and monitoring of trans- GL Q3 2026 Q4 2026
actions within the business relationship.
AMLR 69(3) Formats for STRs and transaction record. ITS Q3 2026 Q4 2026
AMLD 41(2) Criteria for determining whether to appoint a central contact point, and RTS Q3 2026 Q4 2026
the functions of central contact points.
AMLD 40(2) NFS Benchmarks and methodology for assessing and classifying the risk pro- RTS Q3 2026 Q4 2026
file of obliged entities and the frequency of reviews of that risk profile
(non-financial sector).
AMLD 50(13) General conditions for the functioning of AML/CFT supervisory colleges RTS Q4 2026 Q1 2027
in the non-financial sector, including terms of cooperation, operational
functioning, the written agreement, conditions for participation of third
country non-financial supervisors and measures to be implemented by
the colleges when groups include credit or financial institutions.
101

ANNEX XI PLANNING ON RTS/ITS/GL
Texts Articles Title Type Consultation Final Draft
Phase
AMLR 9(4) Elements to be taken into account, based on the entity's nature of the GL Q1 2027 Q2 2027
business, including its risks and complexity, and their size, in determin-
ing the extent of internal policies, procedures and controls, in particular
as regards the staff allocated to the compliance functions. Those Guide-
lines shall also identify situations where internal controls should be or-
ganised at the level of the commercial function, of the compliance func-
tion and of the audit function; the independent audit function may be
carried out by an external expert.
AMLD 49(14) General conditions for the functioning of AML/CFT supervisory colleges RTS Q4 2026 Q1 2027
in the financial sector, including terms of cooperation, operational func-
tioning, the written agreement, and conditions for participation of third
country financial supervisors.
AMLA will deliver the RTS in two phases: first financial sector (FS) and second non-financial sector (NFS).
102
